title: Crispy Vietnamese Vinegar Stir-fry
description: This spicy and crispy Vietnamese dish features a marinade with cinnamon to create a cozy and aromatic meal that is also jain-friendly.

ingredients.name: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Regular fish sauce is preferred for this recipe

instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine fish sauce and sugar to make the marinade. Add minced garlic cloves and press them through a garlic press into the bowl.
Cut chicken breast into thin strips and add to the marinade mixture. Let it marinate for at least one hour or overnight in the refrigerator.
Preheat a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add vegetable oil, swirl to coat the bottom of the pan evenly.
Remove chicken from marinade, keeping any leftover liquid. Discard extra marinade.
Add sliced green onions and red bell pepper strips to the hot pan followed by green beans. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until vegetables begin to wilt slightly.
When vegetables are nicely tender, add marinated chicken pieces in a single layer. Cook until chicken is fully cooked through and golden brown on all sides.
Sprinkle ground cinnamon over the top of the stir-fried dish before serving. Garnish with chopped cilantro for added flavor and freshness.
